data.title: Germany to Debut With Triple-Tranche Bund Auction — Market Talk
data.description: The German Finance Agency will conduct an auction selling three government bonds simultaneously for the first time on Wednesday. Bunds with maturities in July 2042, August 2052 and August 2056 will be on offer with a combined volume of 3 billion euros. The heavy supply, with syndicated transaction from France and Ireland as well, could pose headwinds for Bunds, says Commerzbank Research's Hauke Siemssen in a note. "Bunds are holding up fairly well given the massive supply wave, but markets face continued issuance pressure today," the rates strategist says. France plans to launch a new 20-year OAT, while Ireland will issue a new 10-year bond, both via syndication. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korean shares close at record high on chip, auto boost
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI rises, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won strengthens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises
  • For the midday report, please clicknL1N3YF01L

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares notched a record closing high on Wednesday, led by chipmakers tracking a rally in U.S. peers and automakers also extending gains on optimism around robot technologies.

** The benchmark KOSPI ended the session up 30.46 points, or 0.65%, at 4,723.10, extending gains to a ninth session and marking its highest closing level on record.

** The Bank of Korea is expected to hold interest rates steady on Thursday amid concerns about a weak won, according to a Reuters poll.

** South Korea's unemployment rate stood at 4.0% in December on a seasonally adjusted basis, rising sharply from 2.7% in November and marking the highest since February 2021, data showed.

** Among index heavyweights, chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 1.96% and peer SK Hynix gained 0.54%, after the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index hit an all-time high on Tuesday.

** Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were up 1.35% and up 5.15%, respectively, but battery maker LG Energy Solution slid 1.14% and steelmaker POSCO Holdings shed 2.13%.

** Of the total 930 traded issues, 559 shares advanced, while 336 declined.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 387.0 billion won ($261.93 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,477.5 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.09% higher than its previous close at 1,478.8.

** Earlier in the session, the won hit its weakest level since December 24 at 1,479.2.

** South Korea will implement measures to address increasing volatility in the foreign exchange market and find ways to resolve an imbalance in dollar demand and supply, the country's finance minister said.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) fell by 0.8 basis point to 2.997%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 0.4 basis point to 3.419%.

($1 = 1,477.4900 won)

data.title: Korean Stocks Close at New Record High
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.65% to close at 4,723 on Wednesday, extending gains for the fourth consecutive session after falling in early trading, supported by a strong performance in chip and biotech stocks.

Index heavyweights Samsung Electronics climbed 1.53% and SK Hynix added 0.68%, while biotech names including Samsung Biologics (+1.62%), ABL Bio (+0.61%), and SK Bioscience (+0.96%) also advanced.

Other notable gainers were Doosan Enerbility (+3.68%) and Kia Corp (+3.53%).

The index ended at a fresh record high, settling above the 4,700-point mark for the first time, and is now up more than 11% so far this year.

Sentiment was also supported after the government announced plans to invest KRW 235.1 billion in research projects across the semiconductor, display, and battery sectors, with most of the investment directed toward next-generation chips and automotive processors, reinforcing expectations of continued policy support for key technology industries.

data.title: Motilal Oswal starts coverage on India's Canara HSBC Life with 'buy', sees strong growth ahead
data.description:
** India's Canara HSBC Life Insurance Co gains nearly 1% to 143 rupees

** Motilal Oswal initiates coverage on stock with "buy"; PT of 180 rupees, calling it a bank channel-led compounding story

** Brokerage expects co to continue to gain market share as it increases its penetration among Canara Bank customers, scales up cross-selling in the HSBC channel, builds a strong agency channel, and develops relationship with new-age distributors

** With Canara Bank investing significantly in digital tools for customer segmentation, see a strong growth trajectory for CANR going ahead - Motilal Oswal

** Expects co to report a CAGR of 20%/23% in annual premium equivalent/value of new business during FY25-28E; says VNB margins likely to expand by 50bp each year over next two years

** Stock up 34.8% since trading debut in October

data.title: Embassy of the Federal Republic of Nigeria Appoints Distinguished U.S.-Based Economist Prof. Lingyun Xiang as Honorary Consul
data.description:
Beijing, China--(Newsfile Corp. - January 14, 2026) - The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Nigeria in China officially announced today the appointment of Prof. Lingyun Xiang, a world-renowned U.S.-based economist, multi-national Academician, and leading expert in emerging industry finance, as the Honorary Consul for the Embassy.

Due to his current leadership of critical international financial research projects in the United States, Prof. Lingyun Xiang was unable to attend the ceremony in person. His designated special representative attended the Embassy in Beijing on December 29, 2025, to formally accept the certificate of appointment on his behalf.

Letter of Appointment

Deepening Global Strategic Synergy

This appointment marks a significant milestone in the long-term strategic partnership between Prof. Xiang and the Nigerian government, signaling a new era of profound collaboration in financial diplomacy:

May 15, 2024: Prof. Xiang was appointed as the Financial and Economic Advisor to the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

January 20, 2025: He was honored as the Envoy for Nigeria-China Economic and Trade Friendly Exchange, facilitating high-level cross-regional economic dialogues.

Strengthening Leadership in Global Emerging Industry Finance

As a top-tier economist residing in the United States with an expansive international perspective, Prof. Xiang is recognized as one of the world's foremost experts in emerging industry finance. During the ceremony, embassy officials spoke highly of his contributions:

"Prof. Lingyun Xiang operates at the forefront of global innovation. By leveraging his years of deep-rooted financial expertise and resource networks in the U.S.," said Israel Ishaku Kapio, Minister (Public Communication & Education) of Nigeria. "He has introduced international high-standard financial paradigms for emerging industries to Nigeria. He serves not only as a bridge for bilateral relations but also as a key catalyst connecting advanced global financial technologies with emerging markets in Africa and Latin America."

Upholding Multilateral Interests with a Global Vision

This appointment underscores the high regard for Prof. Xiang's professional standing and his influence on global capital flows. As an American-based economist, Prof. Xiang is committed to fulfilling his duties as Honorary Consul based on international conventions and mutual prosperity. He will provide strategic insights for Nigeria's industrial upgrading by utilizing the sophisticated financial service systems and technological innovation resources available in the United States.

Speaking through his representative, Prof. Xiang stated: "It is a profound honor to accept this appointment. In today's highly collaborative global economy, we will continue to promote the cross-border flow of emerging industries within a compliant and efficient framework. This aligns with Nigeria's economic transformation needs and serves the interests of the transparent, free, and innovative international economic order that I have long championed as a U.S.-based scholar."

About Prof. Lingyun Xiang

Prof. Lingyun Xiang is a prominent U.S.-based economist, a multi-national Academician, and a pioneer in the field of emerging industry finance. He has dedicated his career to the study of global capital markets, digital currency economics, and investment models for emerging sectors. A leader within the American intellectual community and a high-level think tank expert, Prof. Xiang frequently delivers keynote addresses at top-tier global financial summits. His contributions to the formulation of international financial rules and industrial implementation have earned him numerous awards and accolades from governments and international organizations worldwide.

Embassy of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, Beijing

2, Dong Wu Jie San Li Tun Chaoyang District Beijing, 100600 People's Republic Of China

nigeria.Beijing@foreignaffairs.gov.ng

https://beijing.foreignaffairs.gov.ng/

Israel Ishaku Kapio

Minister (Public Communication & Education)

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/280190

data.title: Filinvest REIT's Dividend Yield Remains Ample — Market Talk
data.description: Filinvest REIT's dividend yield remains ample at 7.9%, Maybank Securities analyst Raffy Mendoza says in a research report, as he reiterates his buy rating. The trust is expected to benefit from improving office vacancies in Metro Manila this year, the analyst says. Moreover, the occupancy rate of the trust's office assets is expected to improve to 88% in 2026-2028 from 84% for first nine months of 2025. However, the brokerage lowers the stock's target price to PHP3.15 from PHP3.50, citing an estimated increase in other expenses, including straight-line adjustments related to its Festival mall acquisition in May 2025. Shares are unchanged at PHP3.05. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Peter Thiel-Backed Bitpanda Preparing for Frankfurt IPO, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Cryptocurrency trading platform Bitpanda, backed by billionaire investor Peter Thiel, is preparing for an initial public offering in Frankfurt that could value it at between 4 billion and 5 billion euros ($4.66 billion-$5.82 billion), Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The Vienna-based company's IPO could happen as soon as the first half of the year, but no final decisions have been made, according to Bloomberg.
  • A Bitpanda representative said an IPO is one of the options the company is considering, according to the report.

Full story: https://shorturl.at/1Th3c

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: India's wholesale prices rise 0.83% y/y in December
data.description:
India's wholesale prices (INWPI=ECI) rose 0.83% year-on-year in December after a 0.32% decline a month earlier, government data showed on Wednesday.

Economists polled by Reuters expected wholesale prices to rise 0.3% year-on-year in December.

Inflation in December picked up primarily due to an increase in the cost of manufacturing in sectors such machinery, food products and textiles, a government statement said.

KEY NUMBERS

* Wholesale food prices were flat in December compared with a 2.6% decline in the prior month.

* Vegetable prices in December declined 3.5% year-on-year after a 20.23% drop in November.

* Prices of manufactured products rose 1.82% year-on-year in December compared with a 1.33% increase in the prior month.

* Fuel and power prices fell 2.31% year-on-year in December compared with a decline of 2.27% a month earlier.
